text:
Dad × Top Secret × Confession (父×マル秘×告白, Chichi × Maruhi × Kokuhaku) is the 47 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on November 25 th , 2000.

Overview

Gon and Killua set up a computer to use the internet. Mito tells Gon about his father Ging Freecss and hands him a mysterious box Ging left. Gon manages to open the box with his Nen and finds a ring, a tape, and a memory card. He and Killua listen to the tape where Ging introduces himself and welcomes his son to find him if he can.

Synopsis

Mito removes her bandages to reveal her arm has healed. When she goes to grab a bracelet from her drawer, she notices a box in the back of the drawer and takes it out, considering it.

That night, Mito goes to tell the boys dinner is ready, and sees them working on something in Gon's room. They tell her that Gon is going to start looking for his father, and Mito feels apprehensive about it. After dinner, after Killua has gone upstairs, Mito gives Gon the strange box from her drawer, saying that Ging left it there for when Gon eventually became a Hunter. She recounts some of her memories of Ging to Gon from when she was a child, and tells him that Ging hasn't been home in many years. 

Gon's grandmother tells him the story of how angry Mito was when Ging first left Gon with them back when Gon was just a baby, during which Mito told Ging to never come home again. Mito explains that she had been really worried for Gon back then, and so she got custody of Gon herself. Gon tells Mito about his plans to leave, and Mito is initially hesitant about it, assuming Gon is going to leave like Ging and never come back, but Gon assures her that he and Killua just want to go explore the world together. 

Gon asks to hear more stories about Ging, and Gon's grandmother ends up telling stories about Mito's explorations and how she'd always get lost because Ging never wanted to go with her. Later that night, Mito has a glass of wine by herself at her bar, staring at Ging's photo on one of the bar shelves. She recalls a memory of when Ging eventually came and saved her during a rainstorm when she got lost as a child.

The next day, Mito goes out to hang the laundry and one of the towels gets swept away in the wind. She chases it to a riverside, where she remembers more of the night Ging came to find her during the rainstorm when they were kids. While she is gone, the boys find the mysterious box on Mito's computer desk, which she took back from Gon the night prior.

The boys try to figure out how to open the box. After Killua is unable to open it by brute force, he suggests that Gon try using his Nen to open it. Gon tries this, and the outer layer of the box comes apart in several pieces, revealing another box inside. When they look at a piece that came off, they notice an inscription on the back of it. The box inside has a slot in it, so Gon inserts his Hunter License and the box immediately opens up. The box contains a ring, a memory card, and a tape.

They play the tape while recording a copy of it. Ging speaks directly to Gon on the tape, saying that he doesn't want to see Gon, because he wouldn't know what to say after being selfish and neglecting him for so long, but doesn't actually apologize to Gon for what he did. Instead, he tells Gon that if he wants to find Ging, he'll have to hunt him down. Ging, on the tape, is about to play a recording of Gon's mother's voice, but Gon stops the tape before they hear it.
